blocks:
Ayahs

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْمُزَّمِّلُ (۱)
O you wrapped up in your mantle! (1)
قُمِ ٱلَّيْلَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (۲)
Stand vigil through the night, except for a little [of it], (2)
نِّصْفَهُۥٓ أَوِ ٱنقُصْ مِنْهُ قَلِيلًا (۳)
a half, or reduce a little from that (3)
أَوْ زِدْ عَلَيْهِ وَرَتِّلِ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ تَرْتِيلًا (٤)
or add to it, and recite the Quran in a measured tone. (4)
إِنَّا سَنُلْقِى عَلَيْكَ قَوْلًا ثَقِيلًا (٥)
Indeed soon We shall cast on you a weighty discourse. (5)

Key Points

Muzzammil (مُزَّمّل) means wrapping a garment or cloth around oneself. Some have interpreted it as the mantle of prophethood, while others have said: the intention is that the mission (Risalah) is not compatible with isolation, seclusion, and withdrawal. Some have said: the intention is that the mission is not compatible with rest, and you must rise (in prayer/action).

Tartīl (ترتیل) means reading the Quran slowly, correctly, and without haste. Imam Ali عليه السلام said: Articulate the words of the Quran completely, do not read it like poetry, and do not separate or scatter them. Through its recitation, make your hearts tremble, and let your goal not be merely reaching the end of the Surah. (75)

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Tartīl is that when reciting the Quran, whenever you reach the verses about Paradise, you ask Allah for Paradise, and whenever you reach the verses about Hellfire, you seek refuge in Allah. And in another narration, He said: Tartīl is that the Quran be read with a beautiful voice. (76)

According to the late Tayyib in Tafsir Atyab al-Bayan, the intention behind the "heavy word" (qawlan thaqīlan) is the declaration of the Caliphate and Imamate of Hadrat Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام, which was difficult for the Prophet, and it does not refer to the Quran, because [the verse] says: Read the Quran so that you may receive the heavy word in the future. Thus, the Quran existed before the heavy word.

The Quran is a weighty speech, such that if it were revealed upon a mountain, it would shatter it into pieces. «لو انزلنا هذا القرآن على جبلٍ لرأیته خاشعاً متصدّعاً من خشیة اللّه» (77) Receiving revelation and implementing the Quran in society is also an extremely heavy task. «انّا سنلقى علیك قولاً ثقیلاً»

Although Hadrat Moses عليه السلام spent forty days and nights in Mount Tur for communion with Allah, Allah only mentioned the nights: «ثلاثین لیلة» (78) Perhaps this is because the night is more suitable for intimate communion (munājāt).

75) كافى، ج ۲ ص ۶۱۴. 76) تفسیر نورالثقلین. 77) حشر، ۲۱. 78) اعراف، ۱۴۲.

Messages

1- The leader of the Islamic society must reduce His rest. «یاایّها المزّمّل قم اللّیل»

2- Night is the best time for worship. «قم اللیل»

3- The Night Prayer has been divinely emphasized. «قم الّلیل»

4- Night worship was obligatory upon the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. «قم اللیل» (Just as He says elsewhere: «فتهجّد به نافلةً لك» (79))

5- Spiritual readiness is necessary for undertaking heavy responsibilities. «یا ایّها المزمّل قم اللّیل... انّا سنلقى علیك قولاً ثقیلاً»

6- Religious leaders must rise both at night for the worship of Allah: «قم الّلیل...» and during the day to awaken the people. «قم فانذر» (80)

7- The duration of night vigil is not restricted. (One half, one third, or two thirds of the night). (We should not put people in difficulty regarding acts of worship; one prescription and one type of medicine cannot be prescribed for all people and all times.) «نصفه او انقصه... او زد علیه»

8- In order for worship to be accompanied by vitality, a portion of the night must be spent resting. «نصفه اوانقص منه قلیلاً اوزد علیه»

9- Performing prayer and reciting the Quran at night is the best means for creating spiritual readiness and gaining energy. «قم اللیل الّا قلیلاً... و رتّل القرآن ترتیلاً»

10- Recitation of the Quran has manners (ādāb), and Tartīl is the first of them. «و رتّل القرآن ترتیلا»

11- The recitation of one part of the Quran becomes a prelude to another part. «و رتّل القرآن... سنلقى علیك قولاً ثقیلاً»

12- In management, do not always assume conditions are uniform; the leader must anticipate heavy tasks from the outset. «سنلقى علیك قولاً ثقیلاً»

79) اسراء، ۸۲ 80) مدّثر، ۲.

Ayahs

إِنَّ نَاشِئَةَ ٱلَّيْلِ هِىَ أَشَدُّ وَطْـًٔا وَأَقْوَمُ قِيلًا (٦)
Indeed the watch of the night is firmer in tread and more upright in respect to speech, (6)
إِنَّ لَكَ فِى ٱلنَّهَارِ سَبْحًا طَوِيلًا (۷)
for indeed during the day you have drawn-out engagements. (7)
وَٱذْكُرِ ٱسْمَ رَبِّكَ وَتَبَتَّلْ إِلَيْهِ تَبْتِيلًا (۸)
So celebrate the Name of your Lord and dedicate yourself to Him with total dedication. (8)
رَّبُّ ٱلْمَشْرِقِ وَٱلْمَغْرِبِ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ فَٱتَّخِذْهُ وَكِيلًا (۹)
Lord of the east and the west, there is no god except Him; so take Him for your trustee, (9)

Key Points

The meaning of «ناشئة اللّیل» (Nashi’at al-Layl) is "rising from sleep," just as we read in a narration from Imam Baqir and Imam Sadiq عليهما السلام: the meaning of «ناشئة اللیل» is rising for the Night Prayer (Salat al-Layl). (81) «وطأ» (Wata’) means treading upon or stepping on something, and in the Ayah, it means stepping onto the path of worship. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, in interpreting «اشدّ وطأ» (Ashaddu Wat’an), said: The intention is rising from the bed while intending none but Allah. (82) «تبتّل» (Tabattul) means cutting oneself off from carnal desires and turning one's attention to Allah. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The meaning of «تبتُّل» is raising the hands during the Takbir (declaration of greatness) in prayer, and Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «تبتُّل» is the state of humility and reverence during supplication before the Divine presence. (83)

The Night Prayer in Narrations The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: The best among you is he whose speech is good, who feeds the hungry, and who performs the Night Prayer when people are asleep. (84) The Prophet said three times to Ali ibn Abi Talib: You must perform the Night Prayer. «علیك بصلاة اللیل، علیك بصلاة اللیل، علیك بصلاة اللیل» (85) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The honor of the believer is in the Night Prayer, and in another narration, He said: The adornment and pride of the believer is in performing the Night Prayer. (86) A person said to Hadrat Ali عليه السلام: I have been deprived of the success (Tawfiq) of performing the Night Prayer! The Imam عليه السلام replied: «قَیَّدتك ذنوبك» Your sins have shackled you. (87) Allah addressed Hadrat Musa (Moses) عليه السلام: O Musa, he lies who claims to love Me, yet when night falls, he goes to sleep instead of conversing with Me. (88) The Prophet of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: Two units (rak'ahs) of prayer in the heart of the night are dearer to Me than the world and everything in it. (89) In narrations, the Night Prayer is mentioned as a factor for increasing sustenance (rizq), settling debts, removing worry and grief, and brightening the eyes. (90) Based on narrations, the Night Prayer causes the forgiveness of the sins committed during the day. (91) Furthermore, the Night Prayer is considered a cause for physical health and receiving Divine Mercy. (92) Allah has stated the reward for some deeds in the Quran, but regarding the Night Prayer, He says: No soul knows what Allah has stored up for him: «فلا تعلم نفس ما اخفى لهم...» (93)

81) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan. 82) Kafi, vol. 3, p. 446. 83) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 84) Bihar al-Anwar, vol. 87, p. 142. 85) Wasā’il al-Shi‘ah, vol. 5, p. 268. 86) Bihar al-Anwar, vol. 87, p. 140. 87) Bihar al-Anwar, vol. 80, p. 128. 88) I‘lam al-Din Daylami, p. 263. 89) Ilal al-Sharā’i‘, p. 363. 90) Safinat al-Bihar, vol. 2, p. 46. 91) Furū‘ al-Kafi, vol. 3, p. 366. 92) Bihar, vol. 84, p. 144. 93) Surah As-Sajdah, 17.

Messages

1- The element of time is effective in worship. «ناشئة اللیل» 2- Some graces and blessings originate and stem from the night. «ناشئة اللیل» 3- The darkness of the night provides a better setting for worship free from ostentation and accompanied by humility and reverence. «ناشئة اللیل» 4- Nightly worship has a more lasting effect on the soul. «اشدّ وطأً» 5- The Night Prayer becomes a factor for the steadfastness and strengthening of the believer's faith. «اشدّ وطاً واقوم قیلاً» 6- Even the Prophet is in need of remembering Allah and supplicating to Him. «اذكر اسم ربّك و تبتّل الیه» 7- Success during the day requires gaining strength through nightly supplications. «قم اللیّل... ان لك فى النهار سبحاً طویلا» 8- The Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah necessitates that we always remember Him. «واذكر اسم ربّك» 9- In attaining perfections, aim for the highest degree. «تبتّل الیه تبتیلاً» 10- Nightly worship and daily remembrance are a prelude to complete devotion (inqita') toward Allah. «تبتّل الیه تبتیلاً» 11- Allah's Lordship over the East and the West is proof of His Oneness and the necessity of relying upon Him. «ربّ المشرق والمغرب لا اله الاّ هو فاتّخذه وكیلاً»

Ayahs

وَٱصْبِرْ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَٱهْجُرْهُمْ هَجْرًا جَمِيلًا (۱٠)
and be patient over what they say, and distance yourself from them in a graceful manner. (10)
وَذَرْنِى وَٱلْمُكَذِّبِينَ أُو۟لِى ٱلنَّعْمَةِ وَمَهِّلْهُمْ قَلِيلًا (۱۱)
Leave Me [to deal] with the deniers, the opulent, and give them a little respite. (11)
إِنَّ لَدَيْنَآ أَنكَالًا وَجَحِيمًا (۱۲)
Indeed with Us are heavy fetters and a fierce fire, (12)
وَطَعَامًا ذَا غُصَّةٍ وَعَذَابًا أَلِيمًا (۱۳)
and a food that chokes [those who eat it], and a painful punishment [prepared for] (13)
يَوْمَ تَرْجُفُ ٱلْأَرْضُ وَٱلْجِبَالُ وَكَانَتِ ٱلْجِبَالُ كَثِيبًا مَّهِيلًا (۱٤)
the day when the earth and the mountains will quake, and the mountains will be like dunes of shifting sand. (14)

Key Points

Hajr (هَجر) and Hijran (هِجران) mean separation from another person and withdrawal, whether the separation is physical or emotional (by the heart). Ni'mah (نِعمت) with a kasrah (vowel sound 'i') on the nūn, means something that Allah has given to man, while Na'mah (نَعمت) with a fathah (vowel sound 'a') on the nūn, means enjoyment, luxury, and pleasure. Ankāl (اَنكال) is the plural of Nikl (نِكل), meaning inability or weakness. Nukūl kardan (to refuse/fail) means becoming unable to pay a debt or perform a task. Ankāl refers to the shackles and chains that render a person unable to move. It is narrated in traditions that toothache made Imam Ali عليه السلام restless. He said: O God, all this pain is for a piece of bone, so what will the torment of the Hereafter be? He then recited these verses (94): «انّ لدینا انكالاً و جحیماً و طعاماً ذا غصة و عذاباً الیماً» (Indeed, with Us are shackles and a blazing fire, and food that chokes, and a painful punishment.) Kathīb (كَثیب) means a mound of sand, and Mahīl (مَهیل) means flowing or shifting sand. Turning away and withdrawing from opponents does not mean permanent abandonment or giving up the goal, but rather a temporary tactic in the stages of propagation (Da'wah). As we read in Sūrat An-Nisā': «فاعرض عنهم و عظهم» (So turn away from them and advise them) (95). The threat in this verse, «ذرنى والمكذّبین» (Leave Me and the deniers), may refer to the defeat of the polytheists in battle against the believers, or the divine punishment in the Hereafter.

94) Tafsīr Kanz ad-Daqā’iq. 95) An-Nisā’, 63.

Messages

  1. The remembrance of Allah, cutting ties with others (in devotion), and reliance upon Allah are the foundations for patience against hardships. «واذكر... و تبتّل... فاتّخذه وكیلاً واصبر»
  2. Patience and broad-mindedness are important conditions for leadership. «واصبر على ما یقولون»
  3. A leader must anticipate all kinds of verbal abuse and unjust accusations. «واصبر على ما یقولون»
  4. Sometimes it is necessary for the leader of the Islamic community to boycott individuals or groups and withdraw from them. «واهجرهم...»
  5. One must first be patient and then withdraw. Patience precedes Hajr (withdrawal). «و اصبر... وَاهجر»
  6. Whoever chooses Allah as his Trustee (Wakīl) can easily withdraw from others. «فاتّخذه وكیلاً... واهجرهم...»
  7. Dismissing opponents must be done in a beautiful manner befitting the status of the leader of the Islamic community. «هجراً جمیلاً»
  8. Whoever takes Allah as his Trustee leaves the slanderers to Him. «فاتّخده وكیلاً... واهجرهم و ذرنى والمكذّبین»
  9. Allah consoles His Prophet against the enemies' nonsense. «ذرنى و المكذّبین»
  10. What compels a person to patience and encourages him is the unwavering divine support. «واصبر... ذرنى و المكذّبین»
  11. Usually, the deniers belonged to the affluent and pleasure-seeking class. «والمكذّبین اولى النَّعمة»
  12. If you are unable to confront the possessors of wealth and power, do not worry, for Allah is capable of confronting them. «ذرنى والمكذّبین اولى النعمة»
  13. Granting respite to opponents is a divine tradition (Sunnah). «مهّلهم»
  14. The period of denying the truth and seeking pleasure does not last long, and their opportunity in this world is brief. «مهّلهم قلیلاً»
  15. If Allah grants respite to the pleasure-seeking disbelievers, it is not a sign of His satisfaction, but a delay until the Day of Resurrection arrives. «مهلهم... انّ لدنیا انكالاً و جحیماً»
  16. Disbelievers should not be deceived by fleeting enjoyments, because shackles, a blazing fire, and choking food follow. «اولى النّعمة... انكالاً و جحیماً... ذاغصّة»
  17. Transforming worldly comfort into a painful punishment is an easy task for a Power that turns mountains into sand. «اولى النّعمة... طعاماً ذا غصّة... كانت الجبال كثیباً مهیلاً»
  18. The transformation of mountains into flowing sand indicates the magnitude of the earth's tremor and the earthquake of the Resurrection. «ترجف الارض والجبال و كانت الجبال كثیباً مهیلاً»

Ayahs

إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَآ إِلَيْكُمْ رَسُولًا شَـٰهِدًا عَلَيْكُمْ كَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَآ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ رَسُولًا (۱٥)
Indeed We have sent to you an apostle, to be a witness to you, just as We sent an apostle to Pharaoh. (15)
فَعَصَىٰ فِرْعَوْنُ ٱلرَّسُولَ فَأَخَذْنَـٰهُ أَخْذًا وَبِيلًا (۱٦)
But Pharaoh disobeyed the apostle; so We seized him with a terrible seizing. (16)

Key Points

Wabīl (وَبِیل) means severe and heavy. Shahādah (testimony) and witnessing mean that a person hears or sees something with their senses. According to the verses of the Holy Quran and narrations, the Holy Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) and the infallible guardians (علیهم السلام) are witnesses over all our deeds. The repetition of the word Rasūl (Messenger) in these verses indicates that opposition to the prophets is due to their status as messengers, just as the repetition of the word Pharaoh emphasizes that even the greatest power cannot defend or resist against the wrath of Allah. In the Quran, the Holy Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) is also mentioned as a witness in two other verses.(96) All prophets were sent to their own people, except for Prophet Moses, who was commanded from the very beginning to go to Pharaoh's court and invite him (to the truth). Indeed, in a society governed by an oppressive ruler, the struggle against the Tāghūt (tyrant) is at the forefront of the prophets' mission. (Arsalnā ilā Fir'awn - We sent to Pharaoh).

96) Al-Ahzab, 45 and Al-Fath, 8.

Messages

1- The Holy Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) is a witness over his community and has complete oversight. (Rasūlan shāhidan 'alaykum - a Messenger, a witness over you).

2- The Holy Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) is a witness over deeds in this world. (Shāhidan 'alaykum - a witness over you) and will render testimony in the Hereafter. (Ji'nā bika 'alā hā'ulā'i shahīdan - We will bring you as a witness against these people).(97)

3- The fulfillment of the message (Risalah) is accompanied by oversight. (Rasūlan shāhidan - a Messenger, a witness).

4- The philosophy behind the sending of prophets is the same in all eras. (Arsalnā ilaykum... kamā arsalnā ilā Fir'awn - We sent to you... just as We sent to Pharaoh).

5- History is a mirror of lessons; take heed from it. (Kamā arsalnā ilā Fir'awn fa'asā... fa'akhadhnāhu - just as We sent to Pharaoh, but he disobeyed... so We seized him).

6- In the struggle against evil deeds, go to the source. (Arsalnā ilā Fir'awn - We sent to Pharaoh).

7- Provide practical examples for the promises and warnings you give to people. (When Allah threatens the affluent disbelievers, He reminds them of Pharaoh's fate.) (Dharnī wal-mukadhdhibīna ulī an-na'mah... fa'asā Fir'awn ar-Rasūl fa'akhadhnāhu akhdhan wabīlan - Leave Me and the deniers, those of ease... but Pharaoh disobeyed the Messenger, so We seized him with a severe seizure).

8- Disobeying the command of the Messenger of Allah leads to affliction. (Fa'asā Fir'awn ar-Rasūl fa'akhadhnāhu akhdhan wabīlan - But Pharaoh disobeyed the Messenger, so We seized him with a severe seizure).

97) An-Nisa, 41.

Ayahs

فَكَيْفَ تَتَّقُونَ إِن كَفَرْتُمْ يَوْمًا يَجْعَلُ ٱلْوِلْدَٰنَ شِيبًا (۱۷)
So, if you disbelieve, how will you avoid the day, which will make children white-headed, (17)
ٱلسَّمَآءُ مُنفَطِرٌۢ بِهِۦ ۚ كَانَ وَعْدُهُۥ مَفْعُولًا (۱۸)
and wherein the sky will be rent apart? His promise is bound to be fulfilled. (18)
إِنَّ هَـٰذِهِۦ تَذْكِرَةٌ ۖ فَمَن شَآءَ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ سَبِيلًا (۱۹)
This is indeed a reminder. So let anyone who wishes take the way toward his Lord. (19)

Key Points

Wilīdān (وِلدان) is the plural of walīd (ولید), meaning a child who has just been born, and shayb (شِیب) is the plural of ashīb (اَشیب), meaning an old, white-haired person. Ayah 17 can be interpreted in two ways: A. The severity of the Day of Judgment is such that it turns a newborn child old. B. That Day is so long that a child reaches old age. The word hādhīhi (هذه) in Inna hādhihi tadhkirah... (انّ هذه تذكرة...) can refer to the night supererogatory prayer (Nāfilat al-Layl) mentioned at the beginning of the Surah, just as in Ayah 29 of Surah Ad-Dahr, after commanding prostration and lengthy night glorification, He says: «انّ هذه تذكرة فمن شاء اتخذ الى ربّه سبیلا» meaning, worship in the heart of the night is the path of Allah; whoever wishes may take it.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, there is no escape route from Hell for the disbelievers. «فكيف تتقون ان كفرتم»

2- Those who possessed power in this world were afflicted by severe divine punishment and could not defend themselves, let alone the punishment of the Hereafter! «فكيف تتّقون»

3- Grief and sorrow, bitterness, failures, and hardships are factors leading to premature aging and the whitening of hair on the head and face. «يوماً يجعل الولدان شيباً»

4- The dangers of the Day of Judgment affect both humans: «يجعل الولدان شيباً», and the heavens. «السماء منفطر به»

5- Man has been created free. «فمن شاء...»

6- Becoming aware of the dangers of the Day of Judgment is a cause for remembrance (admonition). «انّ هذه تذكرة»

7- The Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) of Allah necessitates that we choose His path. «اتّخذ الى ربّه سبيلاً»

Ayah

۞ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَعْلَمُ أَنَّكَ تَقُومُ أَدْنَىٰ مِن ثُلُثَىِ ٱلَّيْلِ وَنِصْفَهُۥ وَثُلُثَهُۥ وَطَآئِفَةٌ مِّنَ ٱلَّذِينَ مَعَكَ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يُقَدِّرُ ٱلَّيْلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ ۚ عَلِمَ أَن لَّن تُحْصُوهُ فَتَابَ عَلَيْكُمْ ۖ فَٱقْرَءُوا۟ مَا تَيَسَّرَ مِنَ ٱلْقُرْءَانِ ۚ عَلِمَ أَن سَيَكُونُ مِنكُم مَّرْضَىٰ ۙ وَءَاخَرُونَ يَضْرِبُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ يَبْتَغُونَ مِن فَضْلِ ٱللَّهِ ۙ وَءَاخَرُونَ يُقَـٰتِلُونَ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ فَٱقْرَءُوا۟ مَا تَيَسَّرَ مِنْهُ ۚ وَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتُوا۟ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَأَقْرِضُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا ۚ وَمَا تُقَدِّمُوا۟ لِأَنفُسِكُم مِّنْ خَيْرٍ تَجِدُوهُ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ هُوَ خَيْرًا وَأَعْظَمَ أَجْرًا ۚ وَٱسْتَغْفِرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌۢ (۲٠)
Indeed your Lord knows that you stand vigil for nearly two thirds of the night—or [at times] a half or a third of it—along with a group of those who are with you. Allah measures the night and the day. He knows that you cannot calculate it [exactly], and so He was lenient toward you. So recite as much of the Quran as is feasible. He knows that some of you will be sick, while others will travel in the land seeking Allah’s bounty, and yet others will fight in the way of Allah. So recite as much of it as is feasible, and maintain the prayer and pay the zakat and lend Allah a good loan. Whatever good you send ahead for your souls you will find it with Allah [in a form] that is better and greater with respect to reward. And plead to Allah for forgiveness; indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (20)

Key Points

This Ayah states: Do not become arrogant due to performing good deeds such as the night prayer (Salat al-Layl), reciting the Quran, and attending to the deprived, because you still need to seek forgiveness (Istighfar).

This Ayah demonstrates the comprehensiveness of Islam, ranging from nocturnal tears (of devotion) to daily struggle (Jihad), from the relationship with God to helping the poor, from reciting the Quran to commercial travels, and from performing good deeds accompanied by seeking forgiveness.

In the previous Ayahs, He said: Rise for worship for half, or a third, or two-thirds of the night. This Ayah states: Since calculating this amount of work is difficult and beyond your capacity, Allah has therefore forgiven it for you. Henceforth, rise for worship and perform prayer and recite the Quran as much as you are able and as much as is feasible for you, especially when you are ill or traveling.

Imam Reza عليه السلام, in interpreting the Ayah, «فاقرؤا ما تیسّرمن القرآن» (So recite what is easy [for you] of the Qur'an), narrates from the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله that the Prophet said: Recite the Quran as long as you possess humility (Khushu') and purity of heart. (98)

In this Ayah, travel for the purpose of trade is mentioned alongside travel for the purpose of Jihad, even though the former is a worldly matter and the latter is an otherworldly matter. Based on narrations, if a merchant observes God in his work and avoids short-changing, overpricing, hoarding, and fraud, he has provided for the livelihood of the people and is like a Mujahid who steps onto the battlefield to defeat the enemy and guarantee the security of society. (99)

Although reciting the Quran is not among the obligatory duties, it is highly recommended, to the extent that the command is given twice in one Ayah and is emphasized: «فاقرءوا ما تیسّر من القرآن... فاقرءوا...»

The rulings of Islam are consistent with human capacity and means, and they never entail hardship or difficulty (Usr wa Haraj). For instance, for the sick person, He has prescribed Tayammum (dry ablution) instead of Wudu (ablution), obligated them to perform prayer in whatever state they are able, lifted the duty of fasting from them, and prescribed expiation (Kaffarah) instead.

Now we point to some Ayahs that express this characteristic of Divine commands:

«علم ان لن تحصوه فتاب علیكم» (Allah knew that you would not be able to calculate it, so He turned to you [in mercy].) «فاقرؤا ما تیسّر من القرآن» (So recite what is easy [for you] of the Qur'an.) «الآن خفّف اللّه عنكم» (100) (Now Allah has lightened [the burden] for you.) (100) «اعدّوا لهم ما استطعتم» (101) (Prepare against them whatever you are able to.) (101) «للّه على الناس حجّ البیت من استطاع» (102) (Pilgrimage to the House is a duty owed to Allah by people who are able to undertake it.) (102) «لا یكلّف اللّه نفساً الاّ وسعها» (103) (Allah does not charge a soul except [with that within] its capacity.) (103) «لا یكلّف اللّه نفساً الاّ ما اتاها» (104) (Allah does not charge a soul except [with that] which He has given it.) (104) «فمن كان منكم مریضاً او على سفر فعدّة من ایّام اُخر» (105) (But whoever among you is ill or on a journey [should fast] a number of other days.) (105) «و لم تجدوا ماءً فتیمّموا» (106) (And if you do not find water, then perform Tayammum.) (106) «یرید اللّه بكم الیّسر» (107) (Allah intends for you ease.) (107) «لیس على الاعمى حرج و لا على المریض» (108) (There is no blame upon the blind, nor is there blame upon the sick.) (108)

98) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 99) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur. 100) Al-Anfal, 66. 101) Al-Anfal, 60. 102) Aal-i Imran, 97. 103) Al-Baqarah, 286. 104) At-Talaq, 7. 105) Al-Baqarah, 184. 106) An-Nisa, 43. 107) Al-Baqarah, 185. 108) Al-Fath, 17.

Messages

1- Allah’s knowledge of the conditions of His servants is the best motivation for performing good deeds. «انّ ربّك یعلم انّك تقوم»

2- One of the blessings of nocturnal worship is that only Allah is aware of it, and it is far removed from any form of showing off or hypocrisy (Riya). «انّ ربّك یعلم»

3- The Prophet acts according to Allah’s command. In the previous Ayahs, He said: «قم اللیل... نصفه او انقص... او زد...» (Rise [to pray] the night... half of it, or less... or increase...). In this Ayah, He says: «تقوم ادنى من ثلثى اللیل و نصفه و ثلثه» (You rise [for prayer] less than two-thirds of the night, and half of it, and a third of it).

4- Allah has given man freedom regarding the amount of nocturnal worship so that more individuals may find success in it, and everyone may benefit from its grace according to their capacity. «ثلثى اللیل و نصفه و ثلثه»

5- Rising before dawn was the practice (Sirah) of a group of the Prophet’s companions. «طائفة من الذّین معك»

6- Establishing prayer and reciting the Quran are among the best practices of night vigil. «تقوم فاقرءوا...»

7- Divine duties are easy and do not put man into hardship. «فاقرءوا ما تیسّر»

8- Connection with the Quran and its recitation is necessary in all circumstances, even on a sickbed, or during travel and Jihad. «مرضى - یضربون فى الارض، یقاتلون... فاقرؤا» (If reciting the Quran is necessary during difficulties and challenges, it will be necessary a fortiori under normal conditions.)

9- Earning lawful wealth is Divine grace. «یبتغون من فضل اللّه»

10- Means of livelihood are Divine favor and grace, even if they are acquired through human effort. «یضربون فى الارض یبتغون من فضل اللّه»

11- Mentioning commercial and economic travels alongside travels for Jihad and war indicates the importance of trade from the perspective of Islam. «آخرون یضربون فى الارض یبتغون من فضل اللّه و آخرون یقاتلون فى سبیل اللّه»

12- The manner of lending must be good. (It should be paid quickly, sincerely, and without expecting gratitude or favor.) «قرضاً حسناً»

13- Night vigil, reciting the Quran, establishing prayer, paying Zakat, and lending to others are examples of good deeds. «ما تقدموا لانفسكم من خیر...»

14- Before death, store up good deeds for yourselves, either by your own hand or through a will. «تقدّموا»

15- Good deeds are a provision for the Day of Judgment. «و ما تقدّموا لانفسكم من خیر تجدوه عند اللّه»

16- Good deeds are preserved with Allah and are never lost. «تجدوه عنداللّه»

17- Allah’s rewards are multiplied in every respect. «هو خیراً و اعظم اجراً»

18- No one should consider himself free from shortcomings and failures, because even after performing all duties, we still need to seek forgiveness (Istighfar). «واستغفروا اللّه»

19- Allah has guaranteed the forgiveness of those who seek Istighfar. «واستغفروا اللّه... غفورٌ رحیم»

20- Allah’s forgiveness is accompanied by mercy. «غفور رحیم»
